chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA Const. Petition No. D- 576 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Fresh case. 1. For orders on M.A No. 5523/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection ‘A’ 3. For orders on M.A No. 2016/2024 (E/A) 4. For Hearing of main case. 20.10.2025. Mr. Gulsher Junejo, Advocate for the petitioner. --------- At the very outset, learned counsel for the petitioner submits that father of the petitioner was lawful owner of agricultural land bearing survey Nos.418/5, 419/1,2, 420/1,2,2-A, 421/1 to 3, 445/2, 2-A and 418/3,4 total area 35-33 1⁄2 acres, situated in Deh Kalhora Tapa Markhand, Taluka Shahdadkot District Kamber-Shahdadkot; after demise of his father, the petitioner approached the respondent No.1 for mutation /foti khata badal of land of his father in favour of legal heirs; such entry was mutated in the name of the legal heirs vide entry No.306 dated 140.06.2023. Counsel submits that the petitioner has approached to respondent No.3 for partition on 27.06.2024 but he avoided to initiate legal procedure as prescribed under the law. Mr.Liaquat Ali Shar, learned Addl. Advocate General present in Court in other matters waives notice and submits that the respondent No.2 is required to initiate the partition proceedings as prescribed under the Sindh Land Revenue Act. Learned counsel for the petitioner expresses satisfaction and submits that the petition may be disposed of with directions to respondent No.2 to decide the petitioner’s application in accordance with law. 2 Accordingly, the petition is disposed of with a direction to respondent No.2 to decide the application of the petitioner after providing an opportunity of hearing to the petitioner and all other legal heirs, in accordance with law, within a period of 30 days.

Advocate General present in Court in other matters waives notice and submits that the respondent No.2 is required to initiate the partition proceedings as prescribed under the Sindh Land Revenue Act. Learned counsel for the petitioner expresses satisfaction and submits that the petition may be disposed of with directions to respondent No.2 to decide the petitioner’s application in accordance with law. 2 Accordingly, the petition is disposed of with a direction to respondent No.2 to decide the application of the petitioner after providing an opportunity of hearing to the petitioner and all other legal heirs, in accordance with law, within a period of 30 days. Judge Judge Abid H. Qazi/**
